messages.messageContent: I have created a metric to track the count of sessions that visited the onboarding index page over the last month.
I have created a metric to track session counts for users who visited the onboarding index page and clicked 'Next step' over the last month.

I've set up a 4-step conversion funnel covering the last 30 days:
1. Page visit to https://go.synder.com/onboarding/index
2. Click on the “Next step” button (first time)
3. Click on the “Next step” button again (second time)
4. Click on “Dismiss”

This will show you how many users make it through each stage of that onboarding flow and where any drop-off occurs.

messages.messageContent: I have created a metric for the count of sessions that visited the onboarding page and clicked the 'Dismiss' button over the last month.

Here's a 3-step funnel showing how many users move through your onboarding flow in the past 30 days:
1. Visited the URL https://go.synder.com/onboarding/index
2. Clicked the “Next step” button
3. Clicked the “Dismiss” button

The metric will display the conversion rate between each step so you can see where drop-off occurs during onboarding.

This metric counts how many sessions over the past 30 days (aggregated daily)  
• visited the onboarding page at https://go.synder.com/onboarding/index at least once, and  
• never clicked the “Next step” button in that same session.  
It lets you see day-by-day how many users abandoned onboarding before advancing.

Summarizing information: We have searched for both JavaScript exceptions and network errors specifically for the /onboarding/index page over the past week and found no issues. This confirms that there are no such errors to report for this period.

## JavaScript Errors on `/onboarding/index`

I searched for JavaScript exceptions (errors) occurring on the `/onboarding/index` page over the past week (March 22–29, 2026), sorted by affected sessions. The query returned **zero issues**.

I also ran a secondary search filtering for network errors on the same page, which similarly returned **no results**.

### What This Means

There are no untriaged JavaScript errors or network errors tracked by LogRocket on `https://go.synder.com/onboarding/index` in the last 7 days. A few possible explanations:

- **The page is error-free** — no client-side JavaScript exceptions were thrown during user sessions on this page in the past week.
- **Low traffic** — if few users visited this page recently, there may not have been enough sessions to surface issues.
- **Errors may already be triaged** — the default query only returns untriaged issues. If your team has already triaged errors on this page, they wouldn't appear in these results.
- **Errors may be older** — if the errors you're looking for occurred more than a week ago, they'd fall outside the default search window.

### Recommended Next Steps

1. **Expand the time range** — ask me to look further back (e.g., last 30 days) if you suspect errors occurred earlier.
2. **Include triaged issues** — if your team may have already categorized these errors, I can search across all triage statuses.
3. **Check broader error types** — I can also look for rage clicks, dead clicks, or other UX friction signals on this page if you'd like a wider view of user issues.

Let me know if you'd like me to adjust the search criteria!
